sections.paragraph:
ἑκατονταετής, ές (so rightly L., W-H. [s. B-D-F §13; Lob., Phryn. p. 406f]; mostly [all oth. edd.] accented ἑκατονταέτης) a hundred years old (since Pind., P. 4, 502; Gen 17:17; Philo, Mut. Nom. 1) ἑ. που ὑπάρχων about 100 yrs. old Ro 4:19.—DELG s.v. ἑκατόν.

  1. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

edd. edd. = editions, editors

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 298
